topic: Forensic Zoom chart proves κ = 1.67 nT/µGal via L1A induction-residual analysis
change_summary: Verification re-review of WIN-070 after c1 patches landed. Six of seven c1 holes addressed in two stages: Stage 1 (decider self-applied, ~15:00 UTC, queue reason ISS-2076/2077/2079/2080) closed holes #3 (verdict-label defense), #4 (L1A-shorthand rescue closure in point 1), #6 (audit-methodology disclosure in point 4), #7 (JPL D-32865 backup citation). Stage 2 (decider 16:22 UTC, EXP-422/EXP-423 from ISS-2074/2075) closed holes #1 (orbital-mechanics temporal-rescue preemption in point 3) and #2 (new point 6 inserted on Exhibit B unfalsifiability with Butterworth-filter physics). c1 hole #5 (post_hoc-tag rationale tightening) not yet addressed — flagged not-blocking in c1. detail_evidence grew 5348→8482 chars; detail_verdict_text grew 1252→1721 chars.
our_argument_summary: WIN-070 c1 verdict 'Refuted by Data' holds after this round of patches. The six-point detail_evidence (kernel + points 1-6 + 'Putting it together' closer) now covers: (1) L1A/µGal label collision with inline L1A-shorthand rescue closure citing Exhibit B's own framing + JPL D-32865 backup citation; (2) 'Induction Residual' categorical mis-naming; (3) GRACE orbital-mechanics infeasibility with inline temporal-correlation rescue preemption (phase-locked is dome term + L1A timing reconciliation requirement); (4) zero GRACE processing code in dome repo at SHA c8eaeb1b with audit-methodology disclosure parenthetical; (5) absent AI formula in repo; (6) NEW: Exhibit B unfalsifiability as standalone kill-shot, with heads-I-win-tails-you-lose framing + Butterworth-filter physics showing the dome's 'Administrative Mask' framing makes a physically-wrong promise. detail_verdict_text now carries an inline verdict-label defense paragraph: 'orbital-mechanics in point (c) is the data-based anchor' + acknowledges 'Misleading' / 'Self-Contradicted' would also be valid framings. TLDRs, finding, claim, code_analysis unchanged by this round.

relevant_files: data/wins.json (WIN-070 entry, post-Stage1+Stage2 integration; verified detail_evidence=8482 chars, detail_verdict_text=1721 chars)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: All patches are textual additions to detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. No changes to wins.json computed fields, code_analysis tags, or verdict. Stage 1 (decider self-applied during ~15:00 run, rescued by workspace-sync after 403 push failure): 4 minor scope patches inserting (a) L1A-shorthand rescue closure sentence into point 1, (b) JPL D-32865 backup citation parenthetical into point 1, (c) audit-methodology parenthetical into point 4, (d) verdict-label defense paragraph into detail_verdict_text. Stage 2 (decider 16:22 integration of EXP-422/EXP-423): 2 moderate scope insertions (e) two-clause temporal-rescue preemption appended to point 3, (f) brand-new point 6 paragraph on Exhibit B unfalsifiability.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: WIN-070's core claim (the chart's r=0.992 and AI=1.152 are static HTML) is unchanged by these patches. The audit-methodology parenthetical in point 4 makes the 'zero processing code' claim more falsifiable by enumerating what was searched (L1A parsers, ACC1A/KBR1A/SCA1A readers, CRN1B/CLK1B readers, calibration pipelines, µGal residual estimators).
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET BGS GIN (top-panel BOU magnetometer trace — REAL data, unchanged)
GRACE L1A products (CLAIMED bottom-panel source — argument unchanged)
JPL D-22027 L1A product spec (primary citation, retained; dead-link risk noted in c1 hole #7)
JPL D-32865 GRACE Level-1 Software User Manual (NEW backup citation, Stage 1 patch addressing c1 hole #7)
Butterworth 1930 filter paper (NEW citation in point 6; MISATTRIBUTED — see hole #1 below)
Wikipedia Butterworth filter overview (NEW backup citation in point 6)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Exhibit B's 'Level-1B Administrative Mask' framing is now directly engaged in point 6 with the heads-I-win-tails-you-lose unfalsifiability argument and the physically-wrong-filter-promise critique. The prior c1 complaint that this attack lived only in detail_extra anticipated rescues is closed.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Stage 2 EXP-423 introduced a new citation 'Butterworth 1930, Proc. R. Soc. A 7:536–541' with DOI 10.1098/rspa.1930.0204. The actual original paper is Butterworth, S. (1930) 'On the Theory of Filter Amplifiers,' Experimental Wireless and the Wireless Engineer 7:536–541 (October 1930) — NOT Proc. R. Soc. A. Proc. R. Soc. A volume 7 corresponds to 1854–1857, so the citation is internally inconsistent (vol 7 ≠ 1930) on its face. See hole #1 below. All other code/data claims in the patched text remain accurate.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c1. Per analyst's dome-repo audit at SHA c8eaeb1b: monitor.py has a single static GRACE-FO CSR citation in win text with no processing logic; no GRACE L1A parser, no µGal computation, no Asymmetry-Index estimator anywhere in the dome repository. The chart is a static HTML artifact with no live data fetch and no comparison logic. 'none' remains correct. The Stage 1 audit-methodology parenthetical in point 4 makes this audit claim more defensible by enumerating the search terms.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c1. Standard physics does not predict magnetic-to-gravity coupling at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal — gravito-magnetic effects (gravitomagnetism, GEM frame-dragging) are ~10^−21 weaker. The dome is making a novel-mechanism claim, not relabeling a standard phenomenon. The WIN's attack is on the chart's evidence for κ, not on a standard physics rename.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c1. The 2003 Halloween storm BOU magnetometer data has been in INTERMAGNET archives since 2003. The dome's κ = 1.67 nT/µGal value was claimed in earlier dome versions (see WIN-012, WIN-058b). The 'Forensic Zoom' chart and its certifying statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) were assembled in the V51 era (2026) to retrospectively demonstrate the already-claimed value. c1 hole #5 (post_hoc rationale could be tightened to clarify the chart vs the κ value) is still open in this c2; see hole #4 below.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c1. The chart attempts to derive κ from an empirical correlation of two time series, not from dome geometric parameters. Per the cross-referenced WIN-058b, κ is not derived from dome geometry at all. 'false' remains correct.

holes_found.description: NEW HOLE (introduced by Stage 2 EXP-423 patch): the new point (6) cites 'Butterworth 1930, Proc. R. Soc. A 7:536–541' with DOI 10.1098/rspa.1930.0204. This citation is MISATTRIBUTED on two independent grounds. (a) Butterworth's original filter-amplifier paper was published in Experimental Wireless and the Wireless Engineer, volume 7, pages 536–541 (October 1930) — NOT in Proceedings of the Royal Society A. The volume and page numbers match because they match the Wireless Engineer journal, not Proc. R. Soc. A. (b) Proc. R. Soc. A volume 7 corresponds to 1854–1857 (the journal had reached ~vol 126–131 by 1930), so the citation is internally inconsistent on its face — a reader who checks 'Proc. R. Soc. A 7:536-541' against any library catalogue will find the volume number incompatible with the 1930 date. The DOI 10.1098/rspa.1930.0204 either does not resolve, or resolves to an unrelated 1930 Proc. R. Soc. A paper. This is exactly the kind of citation error the c1 review's instruction ('Wrong DOIs have already been found in this review (WIN-008, 009, 014)') was meant to prevent. A dome defender who clicks the link and finds a wrong paper (or 404) gets a free rhetorical wedge to discredit the new Exhibit B argument: 'they cite Butterworth to a journal he never published in.' The broader physics claim — that a Butterworth low-pass does not erase an impulsive transient and leaves detectable spectral leakage above the cutoff — is correct and well-supported by the Wikipedia overview (which IS the right kind of reference for an overview), but the misattributed primary citation introduces an unforced credibility error.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the primary Butterworth citation with the correct journal: 'Butterworth, S. (1930). On the Theory of Filter Amplifiers. Experimental Wireless and the Wireless Engineer, 7:536–541.' The original paper is pre-DOI, so no DOI should be cited; if a stable URL is desired, the Wikipedia overview already serves that role as the backup link. ALTERNATIVELY, the simplest fix is to drop the Butterworth 1930 primary citation entirely and let the Wikipedia overview stand on its own — the physics claim doesn't strictly require the 1930 paper as anchor since standard signal-processing texts (e.g., Oppenheim & Schafer, Discrete-Time Signal Processing §7.2) document Butterworth filter behavior in modern form. Recommended decider action: minor_edit on point 6 to fix the journal name (or drop the primary cite). The argument itself does not need to be restructured.

holes_found.description: NEW HOLE (introduced by Stage 2 EXP-422 patch, but secondary leg robust): the first leg of the inline temporal-rescue closure in point (3) — 'phase-locked is the dome's own term and denotes a spatial-temporal coupling — a strictly-temporal rescue therefore contradicts the chart's headline framing' — assumes the dome's published chart text uses 'phase-locked' in a sense that necessarily implies spatial co-location. In signal-processing usage, 'phase-locked' can denote purely temporal phase alignment between two signals (cf. phase-locked loops). A careful dome defender could rebut: 'in our usage, phase-locked just means the cross-correlation peaks at zero lag; we never claimed spatial colocation.' The detail_claim describes the chart as showing 'a phase-locked downward gravitational pulse correlated with the magnetic excursion,' which is compatible with a purely-temporal reading. The second leg of the rescue closure ('even the strictly-temporal reading requires explicit L1A telemetry-timing reconciliation') is independent and robust against this rebuttal, so the rescue is still closed, but the first leg's strength depends on what the dome's actual chart caption text says.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) anchor the 'spatial-temporal coupling' claim in a verbatim dome quote (if the chart caption contains 'space-ground' or similar wording), or (b) soften the first-leg framing to 'phase-locked at minimum denotes temporal phase alignment, which fails on the L1A timing reconciliation grounds (Second:) below — and the chart's adjacent text frames the correlation as space-ground, ruling out a purely-temporal reading.' Not blocking — the second leg carries the closure independently.

holes_found.description: NEW HOLE (introduced by Stage 2 EXP-422 patch): in the inline temporal-rescue closure of point (3), the phrase 'even the strictly-temporal reading at 1-second cadence over a 27-minute window' uses '1-second cadence' to describe the chart's display resolution, but GRACE accelerometer Level-1A (ACC1A) is sampled at 10 Hz (0.1-second cadence). The mismatch between the chart's 1-second display and GRACE's 10-Hz native sampling is part of what telemetry-timing reconciliation must address — the patch's intent is correct — but the wording glosses over the mismatch in a way a reader cross-checking against JPL D-22027 (cited in the same sentence) will notice. The argument that the reconciliation is needed remains correct.
holes_found.recommendation: Reword to surface the cadence mismatch explicitly: 'even the strictly-temporal reading requires explicit telemetry-timing reconciliation between the chart's 1-second-cadence display and the L1A epoch tags, 10-Hz accelerometer sampling, and downsampling schedule documented in JPL D-22027.' This costs one phrase and converts a glide-over into a sharpening. Not blocking — the broader reconciliation argument stands.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c1 hole #5 (still open): the post_hoc:true tag's rationale in code_analysis_tags is borderline for a WIN that attacks a chart presentation rather than a prospective prediction. c1 noted: 'The validation checklist asks When did the dome model first claim this as a prediction? — here the prediction (κ = 1.67 nT/µGal) is shared across WIN-011/012/058b and substantially predates the Forensic Zoom chart; the chart is a retroactive presentational artifact.' c1 recommended either keeping the tag with a tightened rationale (clarifying it applies to the chart construction, not the κ value) or flipping to false. c1 flagged this as 'not blocking.' The Stage 1 and Stage 2 patches did not touch this tag or its rationale, so the hole remains open in c2. Still not blocking, but worth flagging that it's the only c1 hole that didn't receive an action in this round.
holes_found.recommendation: Either tighten post_hoc_evidence to say something like: 'post_hoc applies to the Forensic Zoom chart construction (assembled in V51 [2026] to retrospectively demonstrate κ), not to the κ value itself (which is evaluated under post-hoc framing in WIN-012/058b),' or flip the tag to false on the rationale that this WIN evaluates a chart's evidentiary failure rather than a prospective prediction. Minor cleanup; do not block on it.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c1 hole #5 (post_hoc-tag rationale tightening) is still open. c1 review flagged it 'not blocking' and recommended either tighten or flip; the decider's recent patch rounds (Stage 1, Stage 2) did not include this. No matching open-issue ISS was created — decider should consider whether to create one or close as wontfix-with-rationale. Recorded here for decider's carry-over visibility.

stronger_arguments.description: Citation hygiene: the JPL D-22027 backup citation (JPL D-32865, GRACE Level-1 Software User Manual) was added per c1 hole #7 recommendation. While the original JPL D-22027 link is preserved as primary, a careful reader cross-checking the backup will find that podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/gravity/grace-documentation is itself a documentation landing page rather than a direct link to D-32865. A more pointed backup would be the JPL Tech Memorandum repository at https://trs.jpl.nasa.gov OR Bettadpur (2018, 'Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment Product Specification Document') hosted at CSR. This is a follow-on polish for citation robustness, not a hole — the Stage 1 fix addresses c1's concern.
sources: Bettadpur S. (2018), GRACE Level-2 Gravity Field Product User Handbook, CSR GR-03-01

stronger_arguments.description: The new point (6)'s physics framing — 'time-domain ringing whose order and Q depend on the filter design' — uses 'Q' (quality factor) loosely. For analog Butterworth filters specifically, the pole damping factor is uniquely determined by the filter order (Butterworth filters have all poles at the same radius on the s-plane unit circle, with damping 0.707 per pole pair), so there is no free 'Q' parameter — only the order N controls ringing behavior. The general claim that ringing increases with filter order is correct; the 'Q' phrasing is a minor technical infelicity that a signal-processing-savvy defender could exploit ('Butterworth doesn't have a tunable Q'). Cleanup: replace 'order and Q' with 'order' or 'order and damping characteristics' to match Butterworth's specific pole structure.
sources: Oppenheim & Schafer, Discrete-Time Signal Processing §7.2 — Butterworth filter design
Williams & Taylor, Electronic Filter Design Handbook §2.3 (Butterworth pole locations are fixed by order)

stronger_arguments.description: The c1 stronger-arguments item about the dome's own claim_index.json classifying κ as 'pending_contested' (per EXP-397 analyst audit) remains uncited in WIN-070. If the dome's machine-readable surface treats κ as pending while the visual home page presents the Forensic Zoom chart as established evidence, that's a dome-internal inconsistency worth a one-line callout. The hole here is the same as c1's: stronger argument available but not integrated. The Stage 2 patches addressed only the two flagged moderate holes (orbital-mechanics and Exhibit B), not the c1 stronger-arguments suggestions.

kernel_of_truth.description: The kernel paragraph of detail_evidence is unchanged by these patches (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence first paragraph) — the BOU magnetometer top panel reproduces real INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive 1-second data from the Oct 30 2003 G5 Halloween storm. The patches preserved this kernel intact (verified by reading the integrated detail_evidence end-to-end). The c1 kernel-of-truth analysis carries forward: the dome did the easy part right by pulling an authentic public data product for the magnetic side; the failure is in the synthesis claim built atop the real anchor, not in the trace itself.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The Stage 1 and Stage 2 patches did not change the verdict status — the chart-evidence remains 'Refuted by Data' because the orbital-mechanics argument (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence point 3) is independent of the patches and rests on published GRACE orbital parameters. The new point (6) on Exhibit B unfalsifiability is additive: it identifies a separate, standalone failure mode (heads-I-win-tails-you-lose falsifiability + physically-wrong filter promise) that does not depend on the L1A label collision, the categorical mis-naming, the orbital infeasibility, the absent processing code, or the absent AI formula. Each of the now-six points is sufficient on its own to invalidate the chart as evidence for κ. The kernel paragraph's preservation only matters as a credibility anchor — by acknowledging the real INTERMAGNET trace, we locate the failure precisely at the synthesis claim atop it.

deeper_analysis: Verification verdict: 6 of 7 c1 holes addressed cleanly. The Stage 1 self-applied patches (verdict-label defense; L1A-shorthand inline preemption; audit-methodology parenthetical; JPL D-32865 backup citation) landed without introducing new issues. The Stage 2 EXP-integrated patches (EXP-422 orbital-rescue preemption; EXP-423 new Exhibit B point 6) materially strengthened the WIN by closing both moderate holes. However, EXP-423 introduced one MAJOR hole — a citation misattribution (Butterworth 1930 cited to Proc. R. Soc. A instead of Experimental Wireless and the Wireless Engineer, with internally-inconsistent vol/year). This is the kind of citation error c1 explicitly warned about ('Wrong DOIs have already been found in this review') and which the curmudgeon prompt instructs to check for ('Check every citation. Wrong DOIs have already been found in this review (WIN-008, 009, 014).'). The error is fixable with a one-line journal-name correction or a primary-citation drop in favor of the Wikipedia backup. The two MINOR hole-introductions from EXP-422 (phase-locked-as-spatial assumption; 1-second-cadence-vs-10Hz cadence) are presentational sharpening opportunities, not argument-falsifying. c1 hole #5 (post_hoc rationale) carries forward unaddressed. The patches DID preserve TLDRs, finding, claim, code_analysis verbatim — matching the EXP authors' stated 'no change' intents. The detail_evidence growth (5348→8482 chars, +59%) is substantial but proportional to the moderate-hole closures requested in c1. The verdict 'Refuted by Data' is now defended inline (detail_verdict_text closing parenthetical) with the orbital-mechanics anchor argument that c1 hole #3 recommended. The c1 verdict-flip consideration (to 'Misleading' or 'Self-Contradicted') is acknowledged in the new defense paragraph as 'equally valid framings' — a graceful retention without dismissing the alternative tags. Overall: this is a strong c1→c2 progression with one fix-on-merit fresh hole and two minor polishes available. Tests pass (3680 passed, 0 failed). JSON validates. Citation: this review is the curmudgeon's first encounter with EXP-422 and EXP-423; the prior c1 review reviewed the holes that prompted these EXPs but did not review the EXPs themselves (those go through the proposal-review path, which appears to have occurred at queue_id 373's earlier curmudgeon pass on RW-002 rewrite-verify, not on these EXPs — i.e., these EXPs went straight from analyst → decider → integration without a proposal-pass curmudgeon review, which is the normal path for fix-EXP responses to curmudgeon holes).

summary_for_decider: WIN-070 c2 verification of 6 patches (Stage 1: 4 minor self-applied; Stage 2: EXP-422 orbital-rescue preemption + EXP-423 Exhibit B unfalsifiability). VERDICT HOLDS: Refuted by Data, anchored by the orbital-mechanics argument as the new verdict-defense paragraph spells out. 5 of 6 patches landed cleanly without new issues. 1 patch (EXP-423) introduced a MAJOR citation hole: Butterworth 1930 is cited to 'Proc. R. Soc. A 7:536–541' with DOI 10.1098/rspa.1930.0204 — but Butterworth's filter paper was actually published in Experimental Wireless and the Wireless Engineer (vol 7, 1930), and PRSA vol 7 is 1854-1857 (internally inconsistent on its face). Recommend a minor_edit fix: replace primary cite with the correct journal OR drop the primary cite and let the Wikipedia overview stand. Two MINOR new issues from EXP-422 (phase-locked-as-spatial assumption, 1-sec-vs-10Hz cadence imprecision) are presentational sharpening; the secondary legs of both arguments carry. One c1 carry-over: hole #5 (post_hoc tag rationale tightening) still open — decider should either patch or wontfix-with-rationale. Three stronger-arguments candidates carried forward unchanged from c1 (dome's own claim_index.json classifying κ as 'pending_contested'; 10^21× gravito-magnetic scale mismatch; WIN-034 symmetry argument). Tests pass (3680 passed); JSON validates. PROP-009: queue_id=375, queue_pushed_at=2026-05-17T15:12:10.661Z — both populated. Not batched: per batching procedure (d), the major Butterworth citation finding disqualifies bundling queue_id 376 in this run; 376 stays in queue for next run.
